nodes.context: A Chicago-based kidnapping gang composed of University of Illinois alumni, led by Theodore 'Handsome Jack' Klutas, active in the early 1930s.
The organized crime syndicate led by Al Capone in Chicago, active in the 1920s-1930s.
The criminal group led by John Dillinger, active in the 1930s.

edges.evidence_quote: Most of the victims snatched by The College Kidnappers were a part of the Al Capone mob.
edges.context: The College Kidnappers targeted members of the Al Capone mob for kidnapping.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1933, the Capone gangsters cornered Babe Jones, a member of The College Kidnappers, telling him to pass along the message of a payoff to stop kidnapping other Capone gangsters.
edges.context: The Capone mob attempted to negotiate a payoff to stop the kidnappings, indicating a cooperative interaction.

edges.evidence_quote: Klutas had connections with John Dillinger and with the Chicago crime world.
edges.context: Klutas, leader of the College Kidnappers, had connections with John Dillinger's gang.
